metadata.title: Consumer Theory II: Duality & Welfare
learning_objectives: Derive indirect utility and apply Roy's Identity
Derive expenditure function and apply Shephard's Lemma
Calculate CV and EV welfare measures

questions.question: For U = x₁⁰·³x₂⁰·⁷: (a) Find Marshallian demands. (b) Derive v(p₁,p₂,m). (c) Calculate v when p₁=2, p₂=5, m=100.
hints: Substitute demands into U to get v

questions.question: Given v(p₁,p₂,m) = m²/(4p₁p₂), use Roy's Identity to find Marshallian demands.
U = x₁⁰·⁵x₂⁰·⁵. Find minimum expenditure to achieve ū=10 when p₁=4, p₂=1.
hints: Set up Lagrangian for min p·x s.t. U ≥ ū
Solve for x^h then calculate expenditure

questions.question: For U = x₁⁰·⁵x₂⁰·⁵: (a) Derive e(p₁,p₂,ū). (b) Verify properties: homogeneous degree 1 in prices, increasing in ū.
Given e(p₁,p₂,ū) = ū·p₁⁰·⁴p₂⁰·⁶, use Shephard's Lemma to find Hicksian demands.
For U = x₁⁰·⁵x₂⁰·⁵, verify: v(p, e(p,ū)) = ū and e(p, v(p,m)) = m.
U = x₁⁰·⁵x₂⁰·⁵, m=200, p₂=4. Price p₁ rises from 2 to 8. Decompose into substitution and income effects.
hints: Find Hicksian demand at new prices, old utility
Substitution = x^h - x^m_old; Income = x^m_new - x^h

questions.question: U = x₁⁰·⁵x₂⁰·⁵, m=100, p₂=2. Price p₁ increases from 2 to 4. Calculate CV and EV.
Explain mathematically why Giffen goods must be inferior. Use the Slutsky equation.
For quasi-linear U = x₁ + 2√x₂, show that CV = EV = ΔCS.
hints: Quasi-linear has zero income effect for x₂
This makes Marshallian = Hicksian for x₂

questions.question: Write R functions for: (a) Cobb-Douglas indirect utility, (b) expenditure function, (c) Slutsky decomposition.
Household with U = F⁰·⁶N⁰·⁴ (Food, Non-food), m=1000, pF=10, pN=20. Government subsidizes food to pF=5. Calculate (a) CV of subsidy, (b) cost to government, (c) is subsidy efficient?
